name: Indian Cuisine
description: Spices, dals, naan, and the dishes from Punjab to Kerala that fill the menu.

questions.q: What is the primary base of the sauce in butter chicken (murgh makhani)?

source.notes: Tomato + butter + cream is the signature.

questions.q: Butter chicken (murgh makhani) was reportedly invented in what decade and where?

source.notes: Created at Moti Mahal in Delhi by Kundan Lal Gujral, originally to use leftover tandoori chicken.

questions.q: What pulse is referred to as 'channa' in Indian cooking?
What kind of bean is 'rajma'?

source.notes: Rajma chawal (rajma over rice) is a staple of North Indian comfort food.

questions.q: What dairy ingredient is the classic cooling accompaniment to spicy curry?
Kheer is India's traditional take on which Western dessert?

source.notes: Slow-simmered rice with milk, sugar, and cardamom, often topped with nuts.

questions.q: From which fruit is the dessert/drink 'aamras' made?

source.notes: Pulped mango, often eaten with puri.

questions.q: What is chapati?

source.notes: Made from atta (whole wheat flour); cooked on a tava (skillet).

questions.q: Basmati and Ponni are types of which staple grain?

source.notes: Basmati is the long-grain aromatic; Ponni is a popular South Indian variety.

questions.q: What style of naan is sweet, often stuffed with coconut, almonds, and raisins?
What ingredient does paneer cheese famously NOT contain (unlike most Western cheeses)?

source.notes: Paneer is curdled with lemon juice or vinegar, making it strictly vegetarian.

questions.q: What is the name of the deep-fried, triangular pastry filled with spiced potatoes or meat?
What meat goes into the dish 'saag gosht'?

source.notes: 'Gosht' = meat (typically goat or lamb); 'saag' = leafy greens like spinach/mustard.

questions.q: From which Indian state does the dish 'sarson da saag' (mustard greens with cornbread) originate?

source.notes: Often served with makki di roti (cornbread).

questions.q: From which Indian state does the rainbow-coconut vegetable dish 'avial' come?

source.notes: Mixed vegetables in a coconut-yogurt sauce; signature South Indian dish.

questions.q: Which Indian beer brand, produced by a brewery founded in 1857, is the country's most popular?

source.notes: audit-corrected 2026-05-09: clarified the brewery (United Breweries) dates to 1857 while the Kingfisher brand itself launched in 1978.

questions.q: Which crunchy fried fritter is made by dipping sliced onions in spiced chickpea batter?

source.notes: Bhaji and pakora overlap; onion bhaji is the classic British-Indian version.

questions.q: From which Indian state does the dough-ball-and-mash dish 'litti chokha' come?

source.notes: Litti = dough ball stuffed with sattu (roasted chickpea flour); chokha = mashed vegetables.

questions.q: Which fiery red curry from Rajasthan is also called 'jungle meat curry'?

source.notes: Lit. 'wild meat' — traditionally hunters' food, made with red chillies, salt, and ghee.

questions.q: What clay-oven cooking style gives both naan and tandoori chicken their charred edges?
